item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: got some mad 1425 vibes from this one. which is good — that SCP is a good one, and so is this.

item.title: Unique and Original
item.content:encoded:
Damn, the Cherished Ones give better speeches than me.

+1. Now excuse me while I go burn any photos that might be cancerous.

item.title: Good job
item.content:encoded: Honestly, this was a fun and creepy read. I especially loved the aspect of PryMaid being retroactive. I also enjoyed the name "PryMaid."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In truth, PryMaid is retro-causal (hence "You're here because your unborn children betrayed you") so I guess I did.

Yay me!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. Anything to keep me off the streets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Glad you like it.

Must admit I was in a rush to get this done as I figured it was so obvious someone else would surely do it.

That's why it only took three years :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No you're thinking of this one.

Easy mistake to make.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OMG you made the Likeness up at the top rotate between image views and it gave me a jump scare. And I like the new ending.

This was already a fantastic skip how dare you make it even better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be a joke scp?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the best series V article I have yet read. The slick multimedia presentation, the so-obvious-it-why-did-no-one-think-of-it concept, the increasingly irrational behavior, the slow build to the cosmic horror, and the kick-ass wham-line finish. Just excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Without meaning to, by invoking a cult of religiousity without theology, you've basically invented Fifthism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Curious", eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok. That's maybe the fourth comment about the last line so I've changed it. Hopefully the new bit is a 0.0000006% less hammy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm curious as to what gay SCPs we have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This would be one of my favorite articles of the 4000s if it wasn't for the part about the researcher's kids at the end. The rest of it is excellent, though.

Edit: With the new edit, it's significantly better. Excellent article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic work! The audio files were very impressive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
surely they're not being used to create likenesses

Don't speak too soon. That put such a bizarrely nightmarish vision in my head, I had to use it. So I've added a little something into the final "Learning Camp" interview…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Men (who) love men.

Marxism–Leninism–Maoism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm almost 100% ignorant of hubs, canons etc

Yeah I'm pretty much the same, I'm not very well-versed on canons and especially GOIs.

I was going to have a reference to different species being sent to different sectors (humans in sector 8, pigs in sector 4, insects in sector 3 and so on) but I thought that would just push it a bit too far.

While I understand why you removed it, it's still a fun idea IMO. Firstly, making it so PryMaid also enslaves non-sapient life forms makes them larger than I'd imagined by several orders of magnitude, which is creepy. It also leaves us with the perplexing question of what PryMaid could possibly want with animals - surely they're not being used to create likenesses, so what do their eternities of servitude and suffering consist of? - which fits in nicely with the bizarre-ness and mystery of the skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ending spoiler I guess

+ show block
– hide block

The ending was a little similar to that one SCP which was a sweatshop for dead people (1700), but the creepy MLM-speak sold this to me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree almost exactly with Hippo. The whole is greater than the sum of its parts, and the bits that work well, work very well indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Compelling and deeply unsettling. Nicely done.

item.title: Time to throw out my iron
item.content:encoded:
My god, I'm scared of household appliances now…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks

No, not a reference to Fifthism (TBH I like to think I'm almost 100% ignorant of hubs, canons etc because I want my creations to be "my" creations, born of "my" imagination).

The "stars" reference just alludes to the alien influence (obvs), but also that PryMaid have their sights set on *all* life (All Life Is The Same).

In the "factory" sound file I was going to have a reference to different species being sent to different sectors (humans in sector 8, pigs in sector 4, insects in sector 3 and so on) but I thought that would just push it a bit too far. So the "All Life is the Same" phrase sort of suggests that instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice, this is even better - and a lot more streamlined - than it was as a draft.

An anomalous MLM is a great idea for a skip, like others have said I'm surprised this hasn't been done before. But yeah, this is a solid and disturbing skip that raises some intriguing (and unpleasant) questions. The posters and the audio logs were great too (although the audio files still aren't showing up for me for some reason, I had to find them in the "Files" section at the bottom of the page, but if they're showing up fine for others I guess the problem is at my end).

BTW are the new lines about stars in the Vegas presentation meant to allude to Fifthism? If they are, it's a neat touch, but even if they're not I liked that you included them, the imagery works well within the presentation.

Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You'll have to explain that like I'm new.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(though I'm surprised MLM anomalies aren't more common)

I dunno, we have some gay ones.

Oh, wait, you mean communist ones. We also have those.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cheers.

I'm surprised MLM anomalies aren't more common

TBH I couldn't find any. And yet they're an exact fit for weird horror: the cult aspects, the bogus science, the shadowy secrecy, the way people's family and friends become just income streams, the way anyone who tries to pull you back is just negative and doesn't want you to succeed…. I had to make PryMaid so over the top because even real MLMs are bloody weird AF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, this is hammy as fuck — and includes a lot of elements we've already seen (though I'm surprised MLM anomalies aren't more common). Nevertheless, for me, it all mixes together into a surprisingly enjoyable soup.

Maybe it's the multimedia art/audio files; maybe it's the tone of the logs — maybe it's all of this being built around the conceit of selling irons (the little Jesus iron killed me), or the conspiratorial tone. Something about it hit all the right notes.

The ending felt like the weakest part for me — after all this weird almost-believable horror, it felt kind of like a forced escalation. I think letting this peeter out instead of ending with a bang could have worked just as fine. That being said, even the 'weakest part' of this was still strong — I was already upvoting once the "brand loyalty" pun kicked in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheers. I wanted this to have a genuine sense of "personality" to it, hence the images etc. I have about five hundred image files just for the PryMaid symbol alone at different stages of development. I'd like to put them up somewhere, just so when someone complains that their scip took a week to do, I can come back with, "that's NOTHING! Look at this…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. Not Pro-Tools, Reason 10 (I can see you backing away and shaking your head). It's not brilliant for audio work but it's ok. Yeah lots of hours creating those, and every second of that was huge fun.

Ummmm that's given me an idea…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually one of the creepiest things I've read on the wiki. The image and audio work is spectacular, and nearly every twist sent a shiver up my spine. Fantastic work!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy hot dogs, this is amazing! You sir have put a LOT of thought into this. I’m a video editor professionally and done sound design, so hearing those audio logs was a real treat. It actually felt like a live recording, even though it obviously had to be pieced together in Pro Tools.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked the juxtaposition of the alien and the mundane. You've got these alien beings that exist outside of time… that look like clothes irons. And they might have utterly incomprehensible goals, or maybe they just want lots of money. It really makes the whole thing that much creepier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes!!!!!!! Thank you so much for actually posting this, this is really good. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, good to see you figured out how to get the images to work. Sorry I couldn't be much help (also a +1).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And then I looked down at the iron and I saw this freaky alien face smiling up at me with its 27 eyes. I thought, if this little bastard isn't a scip I don't know what is.

Cannot unsee, will never iron clothes alone ever again.

Maybe there'll be a follow up tale or sumfink…

Supplement logs! Supplement logs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Shaggy.

I'd like to applaud the level of polish on the advertisements and the audio logs.

Yeah I obsessed over those, it's fair to say. Random Trivia: the tinkly bell motif all the way through the infomercial consists of three low notes, then two mid notes, then one high note - lay them on top of each other and you have a triangle, just like the PryMaid logo, and just like the typical MLM business model..

ETA: God, that sounds sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this deserves rerate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy and visually pleasing. The perfect duo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe there's something wrong with the audio files, as they're not showing up.

Nevermind, seems it's been fixed.

Also, this is very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy hot dear mother of christ!

I'd like to applaud the level of polish on the advertisements and the audio logs. The former is well-constructed and vary wonderfully in composition, and do a great job for setting the tone for what will transpire; the latter are crisp, with one or two choice lines that *really* stand out in a good way.

Loved the pacing here as well, the way in which the investigation unfolds, the turn and reveal, kept me heavily immersed.

And. Fuck. What a wham line. There's something to be said for building a truly horrifying fate-worse-than-death in a place absolutely riddled with them. Excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. I was worried that people might see it and say, "that's an obvious Photoshop" but it isn't. That's actually what they look like.

It's freakish, and alien, but it's also smiley and amiable in a "drooling" kind of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very bizarre. And I think it makes this object stand out for me. Upvoted.

Also I couldn't see that face on the iron at the start. But then I saw it. Oh no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great to see this up! Happy to help! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Zyn. The images have now been downsized - good call.

It's been a heck of a journey.

Yeah it started about three years ago when I was doing some ironing and I burnt myself. Pretty painful. And then I looked down at the iron and I saw this freaky alien face smiling up at me with its 27 eyes. I thought, if this little bastard isn't a scip I don't know what is.

The idea of the "brand loyalty" was a perfect fit, but I had no idea how to make the jump from alien iron to article. Then I read something about multi level marketing companies and "pyramid schemes" and how they're similar to cults (increasingly bizarre rituals, the shunning of loved ones, the idolisation of the heads etc) and it all came together. What better way of worshipping something than [REDACTED]?

The next two years were spent designing logos, trademarks and ultra-long backstories and interviews which ultimately got cut out (as you know initially there was an entire section about such wonders as male maternity hospitals and bizarre octopi/monkey/lamb creatures) - but even though they got cut, they were still massive fun to create. Maybe there'll be a follow up tale or sumfink…

ETA: The whole "male maternity hospital" thing is hinted at in the infomercial - "And, if you're an infertile human male: familial role repositioning!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see this on the site! It's been a heck of a journey. Gotta say, the blend of eerie and familiar (I've definitely seen people talk about attempting to get into this sort of business scheme…) is done really well. The media really sells it.

That said, the two images on the page are currently over 1 MB in size. Maybe shrink those down so they don't load so much/aren't as big an issue for mobile devices with limited data plans? Let me know if you'd like assistance with the images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Without the media this article would have been decent. With the media it's excellent. Very creepy audio.

I like most of the last interview until it starts talking about the interviewer's kids, that bit just didn't do it for me. But the afterlife bit was otherwise pretty wild. I'm loading this into my head-cannon as one of the many afterlives it's possible to wind up in in the Skipverse, alongside the land of the striders and taskforce Omega-0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was unexpectedly disturbing. Congratulations on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's ham. It's pure ham.

No this is my third scip ( the other two are referenced in this one though) plus a few tales. So if you want to see more stuff you can wander across to my Author Page Humdrum writing awaits!

item.title: Re: first to upvote WOO
item.content:encoded: Thanks, and thanks again for the invaluable feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*inhale*
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
On a more serious note, I feel like it might tread the line between cliche and original (I.e. some of the lines came off as a bit hammy to me [especially the last line], I’m not sure), by for me personally its decent enough. What really boosts my opinion is the multimedia aspects; the audio is freakish and the posters are definitely uncanny valley (maybe also a bit on the hammy side imo but I can’t decide). Good work for your (first?) skip! Hope to see some more stuff from you.

item.title: first to upvote WOO
item.content:encoded: So, so happy to see this hit the main site. You put a ton of work and love and truly evil inspiration into PryMaid and it shows! Can't wait to dream up ways to fit those hungry irons into my corner of the SCPF universe. And to see what you'll create next!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huge thanks to the following for various pointers, help etc
Isomers
Zyn
Leveritas
Mayoculpa
Hercules Rockefeller
Croquembouche

All audio is my own.

All images are my own except the following, which are all cc compliant:

"To the Nth Generation"
https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Family_Portrait.jpg

"Famiy Blessings"
http://www.everystockphoto.com/photo.php?imageId=9719165

War (not used)
https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Chisinau_riot_2009-04-07_02.jpg

"All Life Is The Same"
https://stocksnap.io/photo/QUXQEQXVMV

"Agent Zachary"
http://gallery.hd.org/_c/people/_more2007/_more04/male-figure-hirsute-bearded-adult-with-glasses-bare-torso-back-and-blue-jeans-back-1-JR.jpg.xhtml

Bored? Want to be more bored? Head on over to my Author Page!
